_source_note: Approximated from publicly known DEFRA/UK Government GHG Conversion Factors, US EPA emission factor methodology, and Poore & Nemecek (2018, Science) food lifecycle data. Verify exact current-year figures at gov.uk/government/publications/greenhouse-gas-reporting-conversion-factors and epa.gov/climateleadership/ghg-emission-factors-hub before any commercial or compliance use.
_disclaimer: Directional estimates for personal footprint awareness only.

groceries.label: Groceries
groceries.alternativeTip: Shifting even a few meals a week toward plant-based proteins is the single biggest lever on a grocery bill's footprint.

restaurant.label: Restaurant & Dining
restaurant.alternativeTip: Dining at local farm-to-table restaurants or reducing food waste saves transport and landfill methane emissions.

clothing.label: Clothing & Fashion
clothing.alternativeTip: Buying second-hand garments or renting special occasion wear halts clothing production footprint entirely.

electronics.label: Electronics Retail
electronics.alternativeTip: Buy refurbished devices or repair existing hardware to avoid heavy semiconductor manufacturing emissions.

fuel.label: Fuel & Gas
fuel.alternativeTip: Consolidating trips, choosing public transit, or carpooling can slice fuel consumption and direct emissions in half.

pharmacy.label: Pharmacy & Health
pharmacy.alternativeTip: Opting for generic medicines in bulk packaging and eco-friendly personal care items reduces container waste.

homeGoods.label: Home Goods & Furniture
homeGoods.alternativeTip: Choosing pre-owned wooden furniture or certified eco-textiles cuts manufacturing footprint by up to 70%.

general.label: General Retail
general.alternativeTip: Review purchases to prioritize high-durability items and reduce non-essential packaging waste.

alternative.label: Recycled fabric or vegan leather
alternative.message: Recycled fabric and vegan leather alternatives avoid the methane-heavy livestock supply chain entirely.

alternative.label: Organic or recycled cotton
alternative.message: Organic and recycled cotton use less water and avoid chemical-intensive synthetic pesticide application.

alternative.label: Recycled polyester (rPET) or linen
alternative.message: Recycled polyester or natural linen drastically reduces fossil fuel extraction and microplastic shedding.

alternative.label: Reusable metal or glass containers
alternative.message: Replacing single-use plastic with reusable steel or glass lowers lifetime plastic production emissions by over 80%.

alternative.label: Sustainably certified (FSC) wood
alternative.message: FSC-certified timber guarantees responsible forest management that protects active carbon sinks.

alternative.label: Recycled glass or metal container
alternative.message: Recycled glass melts at lower temperatures, saving process energy and carbon emissions.

alternative.label: Recycled aluminum or steel
alternative.message: Recycling metal consumes up to 95% less energy than refining virgin ores from mine to market.

alternative.label: Unbleached recycled paper
alternative.message: Unbleached post-consumer paper preserves forests and bypasses carbon-heavy chlorine chemical treatments.

alternative.label: Refurbished cable/charger or repair
alternative.message: Using refurbished accessories prevents e-waste and halts resource extraction for new circuitry.

alternative.label: Refurbished smartphone or battery swap
alternative.message: Buying refurbished or repairing your current phone avoids the carbon-intensive manufacturing of a new mainboard.

flightFactors.railNote: Rail passenger factor is a broad national average, not specific to route or operator speed
